ctx.name: Carl
ctx.notes: He's not managing the CoE ux/ui enough.

introQueue.label: The real version
introQueue.name: Tell me about your week — the real version.
introQueue.description: Kim Scott's 'care personally' made concrete. Signals that a performed answer won't cut it.

introQueue.label: Self-read opener
introQueue.name: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
introQueue.description: Anchors the conversation in their self-assessment first; gap vs manager's view becomes a signal.

introQueue.label: What they're proud of
introQueue.name: What's something from the last quarter you're genuinely proud of — not what looks good, what actually was good?
introQueue.description: Separates real wins from performative ones; calibrates their sense of impact.

focus_points.label: Impact of UX on strategic goals this quarter.
focus_points.reason: Notes mention insufficient management of the CoE ux/ui. Could indicate a need to clarify how UX initiatives align with broader strategic goals.

focus_points.label: Delegation of UX/UI tasks within the CoE.
focus_points.reason: Notes suggest he's not managing the CoE ux/ui enough. Might be a sign to explore how effectively he's delegating responsibilities within the team.

focus_points.label: Engagement with CoE stakeholders.
focus_points.reason: Notes point to a lack of management over the CoE ux/ui. This could reflect challenges in stakeholder engagement or expectation management.

focus_points.label: Current UX priorities and alignment with CoE.
focus_points.reason: No specific signal — selected because this is a normal Performance & feedback topic for a Lead UX role. Ensures alignment with CoE objectives.

focus_points.label: Support needed to manage CoE ux/ui effectively.
focus_points.reason: No specific signal — standard Performance & feedback hygiene. Opens dialogue on what support he might need from you to manage CoE responsibilities effectively.

brief.coreIssue: Carl, as the UX Lead, is not effectively managing the Center of Excellence (CoE) for UX/UI, which might be impacting the strategic goals for this quarter. This meeting should address how effectively he is delegating tasks within the CoE, his engagement with stakeholders, and the alignment of current UX priorities with CoE objectives. The goal is to understand any barriers Carl faces in managing these responsibilities and what support he might need from you.
brief.openingQuestion: Carl, how do you see the current UX initiatives aligning with our strategic goals for this quarter, and what challenges have you faced in managing the CoE to achieve these?
listenFor: whether he aligns UX initiatives with strategic goals
if they feel supported in managing CoE responsibilities
whether he is effectively engaging with stakeholders
avoid: do not assume lack of effort without specifics
do not overlook his perspective on current challenges
brief.goodOutcome: You will have a clearer understanding of Carl's current management of the CoE and any support he may require.
brief.suggestedAction: Identify one specific area where Carl needs support and agree on next steps to address it within a week.

issues: output may not reflect the meeting type distinctly

queueRef.name: When you say 'my to-do list is just too big', what's on it that's taking the most time right now?
queueRef.description: Drills into specifics of task overload to identify stress points.
